settings.title: Five Years In, a Free Grooming Day for Rescues
settings.excerpt: To mark five years we are opening the salon for a day to dogs waiting for new homes at the local shelter.
settings.body:
Pawlish turns five this month. It has been five years of muddy paws, nervous first-timers, and a great many treats handed out.

When we opened we had two grooming tables and a lot of hope. The dogs, and the people attached to them, did the rest. Five years on it still does not quite feel like work.

A day for the shelter dogs

To say thank you, we are giving a full day to the local rescue shelter. A clean, well-groomed dog is far more likely to catch a visitor's eye, and a fresh coat can be the small thing that finds a dog its home.

What we will be doing on the day:

  • A wash, brush, and tidy for as many shelter dogs as we can fit.
  • Gentle handling for the ones who find new places frightening.
  • A photo of each dog looking its best, for the rehoming listings.

We are closing to regular bookings for the day so the whole team can focus on the shelter dogs, free of charge. Normal appointments resume the next morning.

Thank you

To everyone who has trusted us with a beloved dog over these five years, thank you. Watching those nervous puppies grow into regulars has been the best part of the job, and we hope to do it for many more years yet.
